ai-gateway.title: AI Gateway
ai-gateway.description: Observe and control your AI applications
ai-gateway.onboarding.hero.title: AI Gateway
ai-gateway.onboarding.hero.subtitle: One place to observe, control, and optimize all your AI applications. Connect any provider, track every request, and reduce costs.
ai-gateway.onboarding.customGateway.desc: Want more control? Use a named gateway with custom settings.
ai-gateway.onboarding.codeExamples.intro: A default gateway will automatically be created on your first authenticated request.
ai-gateway.onboarding.codeExamples.token: To run the example, create a token with AI Gateway and Workers AI permissions. You can also run <0>wrangler auth token</0> to get an auth token for use with the API.
To run the example, create a token with AI Gateway permissions and your provider's API key. You can also run <0>wrangler auth token</0> to get an auth token for use with the API.
ai-gateway.gettingStarted.title: Get Started
ai-gateway.gettingStarted.features.title: What you get with AI Gateway
ai-gateway.gettingStarted.features.analy: Real-time cost and usage analytics across all providers
ai-gateway.gettingStarted.features.loggi: Full request logging with prompt and response storage
ai-gateway.gettingStarted.features.cachi: Response caching to reduce latency and costs
ai-gateway.gettingStarted.features.rateL: Rate limiting to control spend and prevent abuse
ai-gateway.banners.logs_limit: Logs now persist and you can store up to 10M logs per gateway. See docs to <0>learn more</0>.
ai-gateway.banners.no_secret_quota: You have crossed the quota for the number of secrets you can create on Secrets Store. Please remove existing secrets to create new ones. <0>Learn More</0>
ai-gateway.banners.no_secrets_store_perm: You do not have permissions to create and deploy secrets on Secrets Store. Please contact your account administrator to grant you the necessary permissions. <0>Learn More</0>
ai-gateway.banners.no_ai_gateway_edit_pe: You do not have permission to add or edit AI Gateway provider keys. Please contact your account administrator to grant you the necessary permissions. <0>Learn More</0>
ai-gateway.banners.unified_billing_expla: Choose how to authenticate with AI providers
Pay through Cloudflare. No API keys needed.
Pay through Cloudflare and skip API key management. Load credits and start making requests immediately.
Currently supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI Studio, xAI, and Groq.
Store your own provider API keys securely.
Securely store your own provider API keys in Cloudflare. Keys are included automatically at runtime.
ai-gateway.intro: Settings here will affect your %{gateway} gateway only. Use multiple gateways if you want to have different settings for different applications.
ai-gateway.note: Workers AI will start billing for neuron usage on April 1st. <0>Learn more</0>
ai-gateway.accountStats.upgrade_benefits: Upgrade to Workers Paid to unlock:
Send your AI Gateway logs to a destination of your choice with Logpush
Create up to 20 gateways instead of %{current}
Store up to 10 million logs per gateway instead of %{current}
Store up to 10 million logs per gateway

ai-gateway.createGateway.description: Choose an name for your gateway.

ai-gateway.createGateway.settings.descri: Important settings for your gateway, you can always change them later
ai-gateway.logs.title: Real-time logs
ai-gateway.logs.no_logs: No logs exist for this gateway during the selected time period. <0>Learn how to send your first request</0>
ai-gateway.logs.no_collect_logs: Logging is currently disabled. To enable it, please adjust in <0>settings</0>.
ai-gateway.logs.error_forbidden: You don't have permission to view these logs.
ai-gateway.logs.error_not_found: These logs could not be found.
ai-gateway.logs.error_overloaded: The logs service is temporarily overloaded and could not return your logs. This is usually transient — try again in a moment.
ai-gateway.logs.error_generic: Something went wrong while loading logs. Please try again.
ai-gateway.logs.example: Or use the code snippet below to quickly test and send your first request.
ai-gateway.logs.search_content: Search in the request or response content
ai-gateway.logs.live_waiting_description: New logs will appear here automatically as requests come in.
ai-gateway.logs.drawer.metadata.title: Edit metadata
ai-gateway.logs.details.error_title: Unable to load request and response
ai-gateway.logs.details.error_forbidden: You don't have permission to view this request log.
ai-gateway.logs.details.error_not_found: This log could not be found.
ai-gateway.logs.details.error_generic: Something went wrong while loading this log. Please try again.
ai-gateway.logs.delete_logs.title: Delete Filtered Logs
ai-gateway.logs.delete_logs.description: Currently we delete 10 000 logs max at a time. If you need to delete more logs, please repeat this step until all the logs you would like deleted are removed
ai-gateway.evaluations.table.header.tool: Percent of thumbs up annotated from logs.
ai-gateway.evaluations.remove_evaluation: Are you sure you want to delete this evaluation?
Deleting %{evaluation} is permanent and cannot be undone.
ai-gateway.evaluations.create_dataset_to: Apply filters to narrow down your logs, then select **Create Dataset** to store the filtered logs for future analysis.
ai-gateway.settings.logs.title: Collect Logs
ai-gateway.settings.logs.description: Store request and response payloads, including the prompt, response, provider, timestamps, and the status of the request.
ai-gateway.settings.logs.limit.title: Set log limit
ai-gateway.settings.logs.limit.descripti: Maximum of %{value} logs stored per gateway.
ai-gateway.settings.logs.limit.note: New logs outside of the set limit will not be stored
ai-gateway.settings.logs.limit.limits: You cannot exceed the limit of %{value} logs. Please update. (min: %{value_min})
When gateway storage limit is reached new logs will stop being saved.
Automatically delete the oldest logs when gateway storage limit is reached to ensure new logs are always saved.
ai-gateway.settings.improved_logs.title: Improved Logs
ai-gateway.settings.log_push.title: Export AI Gateway Logs
ai-gateway.settings.log_push.description: Securely export logs to an external storage location using Logpush.
ai-gateway.settings.log_push.modal.title: Update Public Key
ai-gateway.settings.log_push.modal.descr: Upload the public key you generated to encrypt your logs.
ai-gateway.settings.caching.title: Cache Responses
ai-gateway.settings.caching.description: Serve requests from our cache rather than your original provider to lower costs and provide faster responses for your users.
ai-gateway.settings.ratelimiting.title: Rate Limit Requests
ai-gateway.settings.ratelimiting.descrip: Control the traffic your application gets to cap spending or prevent suspicious activity by limiting requests.
ai-gateway.settings.spend_limits.title: Spend Limits
ai-gateway.settings.spend_limits.descrip: Set cost budgets for this gateway and choose how each budget is shared across metadata, providers, and models.
ai-gateway.settings.spend_limits.confirm: This will delete the spend limit rule. This action cannot be undone.
ai-gateway.settings.spend_limits.no_rule: No spend limit rules are configured yet. Add a rule to start enforcing gateway budgets.
ai-gateway.settings.spend_limits.metadat: Metadata is custom JSON you attach to requests with the cf-aig-metadata header. Use it to split a budget by a key, like each user, or match a value, like a specific team.
ai-gateway.settings.spend_limits.modal.d: Create a cost budget over a rolling window. Optional dimensions decide whether the budget is shared, split per value, or limited to one value.
ai-gateway.settings.spend_limits.modal.p: Shared across all providers by default. Add one or more providers, like fal or replicate, to limit this rule to matching requests.
ai-gateway.settings.spend_limits.modal.m: Shared across all models by default. Add one or more models in author/model format to limit this rule to matching requests. Supports wildcards, like black-forest-labs/*.
Cost limit must be greater than 0.
Window length must be a positive whole number of seconds.
Metadata match dimensions require a value.
ai-gateway.settings.retry.title: Retry Requests
ai-gateway.settings.retry.description: Automatically retry failed requests to improve reliability. Per-request headers can override these defaults.
ai-gateway.settings.retry.resume: Retry up to %{attempts} times with %{delay} delay using %{backoff} backoff.
ai-gateway.settings.rename_gateway.title: Edit Gateway
ai-gateway.settings.rename_gateway.descr: Update the name and URL slug of Gateway %{gateway}.
ai-gateway.settings.cleanup_logs.title: Auto Log Cleanup
ai-gateway.settings.cleanup_logs.descrip: Automatically delete the oldest logs to stay within the gateway's storage limit and ensure new logs are saved.
ai-gateway.settings.security.title: Guardrails
ai-gateway.settings.security.description: Identify unsafe or inappropriate content in prompts and responses based on selected categories, then flag or block as needed.
ai-gateway.settings.security.note: Guardrails runs an additional Workers AI inference on every request. Standard Workers AI pricing applies.
ai-gateway.settings.security.dlp.title: Data Loss Prevention (DLP)
ai-gateway.settings.security.dlp.descrip: Scan content for sensitive data and prevent data leakage by selecting DLP profiles.
ai-gateway.settings.security.dlp.profile: The AI requests/responses will be checked against each of the selected profiles.
ai-gateway.settings.security.dlp.no_poli: No policies configured. Click "Add Policy" to create your first DLP policy.
ai-gateway.settings.security.dlp.action_: If any of the selected profiles match a request, this is the action that will be taken
ai-gateway.settings.security.dlp.check_d: Choose whether to check the request, response, or both
ai-gateway.settings.security.dlp.validat: At least one profile must be selected
ai-gateway.settings.auth.title: Authenticated Gateway
ai-gateway.settings.auth.tooltipEnabled: Authentication is enabled for this gateway. HTTP requests must include an authorization token in the header; Worker bindings are authenticated automatically. <0>Settings
ai-gateway.settings.auth.tooltipDisabled: Authentication is disabled for this gateway. Enabling it will require an authorization token in the header for HTTP requests; Worker bindings are authenticated automatically. <0>Settings
ai-gateway.settings.auth.description: When authentication is enabled, HTTP requests must include an authorization token in the header. Requests made through a Worker binding are authenticated automatically. <0>Create authentication token
ai-gateway.settings.auth.modal.descripti: Before proceeding, make sure all HTTP requests to this gateway include a valid authorization token, as requests without one will fail. Requests made through a Worker binding are not affected.
Turning off authentication will allow requests without an authorization token to access this gateway, and any existing tokens will be ignored.
ai-gateway.settings.zdr.title: Zero Data Retention
ai-gateway.settings.zdr.description: Enable Zero Data Retention mode to ensure AI providers do not retain any data related to requests and responses for unified billing requests.
ai-gateway.settings.zdr.modal.title: Enable Zero Data Retention for %{gateway}?
ai-gateway.settings.zdr.modal.descriptio: Enabling ZDR will prevent AI providers from retaining request and response data. Note that features requiring data retention, such as prompt caching, may stop working when ZDR is enabled. This setting only impacts requests using Unified Billing on AI Gateway.
ai-gateway.settings.workers_ai_billing_m: Choose how Workers AI usage through this gateway is billed.
Standard billing charges your account at the end of each billing cycle. Unified billing deducts from your AI Gateway credit balance in real time.
ai-gateway.settings.otel.title: Otel Integration
ai-gateway.settings.otel.description: Configure Otel integration to automatically report tracing data to an OpenTelemetry endpoint.
ai-gateway.settings.stripe_integration.d: Configure Stripe integration to automatically report usage-based billing data to a meter on your Stripe account.
ai-gateway.settings.stripe_integration.s: Leave empty to keep existing key, or enter new key to update
Create an API key on your stripe dashboard with access to report meter events.
ai-gateway.settings.stripe_integration.p: Configure the format of the meter event payloads sent to your usage-based billing meter.
ai-gateway.settings.stripe_integration.d: AI Gateway will send default meter event payloads formats for LLM tokens to your meter.
Requests need to pass a valid Stripe customer ID in the "customerId" field on custom metadata in their AI Gateway request using the following header:
AI Gateway will send meter events for token usage to your Stripe account.
ai-gateway.settings.stripe_integration.c: Stripe is configured with %{count} custom usage event(s)
Stripe integration is configured and active
ai-gateway.settings.stripe_integration.t: $cost - Total cost of the request in USD
$metadata_metadataKey - Custom metadata field from cf-aig-metadata header
Replace 'metadataKey' with your actual field name (e.g., $metadata_customerId)
ai-gateway.settings.stripe_integration.m: This will disable Stripe integration and remove your stripe API key from secret store. This action cannot be undone.
ai-gateway.settings.stripe_integration.e: At least one usage event payload is required
Failed to save Stripe key to secure storage
Failed to update Stripe integration settings
Failed to remove Stripe integration settings

ai-gateway.settings.remove_gateway.descr: Deleting Gateway %{gateway} is permanent and cannot be undone. You will no longer be able to send any requests through it, and all logs and metrics related to it will be lost.
ai-gateway.settings.remove_gateway.modal: Deleting %{gateway} is permanent and cannot be undone. You will no longer be able to send any requests through it, and all logs and metrics related to it will be lost.

ai-gateway.datasets.title: Datasets
ai-gateway.datasets.description: Datasets are collections of logs stored for analysis that can be used for an evaluation

ai-gateway.datasets.modal_create.title: Create Dataset
ai-gateway.datasets.modal_create.descrip: Choose an identifier for your dataset.
ai-gateway.routing.name: Dynamic Routing
ai-gateway.routing.description: Route each request to an LLM best fit for the job. Enforce rate limits, spend caps and custom conditional rules. Split traffic by percentage, target specific models or providers, and automatically fail over on errors.
ai-gateway.routing.example.card_title: Get started with an example route
ai-gateway.routing.example.card_descript: Choose a template to get started quickly, or build from scratch.
ai-gateway.routing.example.modal_descrip: Choose a template to get started quickly, or build from scratch.
ai-gateway.routing.example.template_desc: Route to gpt-4o with automatic Workers AI fallback
ai-gateway.routing.example.blank_descrip: Build your own routing logic from scratch
ai-gateway.routing.example.error: Failed to create route. Please check the route name and try again.
ai-gateway.routing.delete_route.title: Confirm
ai-gateway.routing.delete_route.message: Are you sure you want to delete this route?
ai-gateway.routing.delete_route.error: Failed to delete route. Please try again.
ai-gateway.routing.save_version.invalid_: Route has validation errors. You can save, but deploying is disabled until errors are fixed.
ai-gateway.routing.save_version.save_err: Failed to save version. Please try again.
ai-gateway.routing.save_version.deploy_e: Failed to deploy version. Please try again.

This will deploy version %{versionId} and replace the currently deployed configuration. Are you sure you want to continue?

ai-gateway.routing.raw_schema.dialog_des: Copy the schema to share with support, or as a starting point for patching the route via the REST API. The dash builder doesn't currently support editing the JSON directly — see the <0>JSON configuration docs</0> for the supported element shapes.
ai-gateway.routing.unsupported_node.desc: Managing %{type} is not currently supported via the UI. Edit this route via the <0>JSON configuration</0> or the <1>REST API</1>.

ai-gateway.new_user.title: Get started by creating an AI Gateway
ai-gateway.new_user.description: AI Gateway lets you tunnel your AI requests through Cloudflare so you can get logs, caching, rate-limiting and more. Use different gateways to separate logs, metrics and settings between different applications. You can create as many gateways as you want. Start by creating your first one!
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_1.name: Choose dataset
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_1.title: Choose or create dataset
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_1.descrip: Datasets are the collection of logs that will be used for this evaluation. Choose from your existing datasets or create a new one.
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_2.name: Choose evaluators
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_2.title: Choose evaluators
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_2.descrip: Choose the evaluators you’d like to include. By default all evaluations include cost and speed.
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_3.name: Review & run
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_3.title: Review & Run
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_3.descrip: Take one last look over your evaluation, and make any changes
ai-gateway.new_evaluation.step_3.evaluat: Used to reference your evaluation in the dashboard

ai-gateway.overview.gateway_endpoint.des: Make requests directly to this endpoint with curl or any HTTP client. For OpenAI SDKs, use the base URL (up to and including /compat) as your baseURL. The SDK appends the path automatically.
ai-gateway.overview.quick_start.title: Quick Start with Unified Billing
ai-gateway.overview.quick_start.no_api_k: No provider API key needed - charges deducted from your Cloudflare credits.

ai-gateway.errors.provider_keys.internal: Internal error occurred. Please contact customer support for assistance.
ai-gateway.errors.provider_keys.invalid_: Invalid JSON format. Please check your input.
ai-gateway.credits.title: Credits
ai-gateway.credits.add_payment_error: Add a payment method to purchase credits
ai-gateway.credits.topup_error_stripe_no: Stripe has not loaded yet. Please try again.
ai-gateway.credits.topup_error_card_decl: We could not complete your payment. Your card may have expired or been declined. Please update your payment method in the Billing section and try again.
ai-gateway.credits.topup_error_requires_: Your payment requires additional authentication that was not completed. Please try again or use a different payment method.
ai-gateway.credits.topup_error_processin: Your payment is still processing. Please check back shortly to confirm your credits.
ai-gateway.credits.topup_error_generic: Your payment did not complete and no credits were added. Please try again.
ai-gateway.credits.topup_error_unexpecte: Failed to process payment. Please try again.
ai-gateway.credits.paypal_only_error: An active credit or debit card is required to purchase AI Gateway credits.
ai-gateway.credits.paypal_primary_error: AI Gateway credits require a credit or debit card as your primary payment method. Set your card as primary to continue.
ai-gateway.credits.no_billing_permission: You do not have permission to manage billing for this account. Contact your account administrator to request access.
ai-gateway.credits.topup_description: You can purchase credits using your stored payment methods. These can be utilized to against any supported provider-model combinations via Cloudflare AI Gateway.
ai-gateway.credits.autotopup_description: Add credits automatically when they crosses a certain threshold. Enabling auto top-up is recommended to avoid availability disruptions.
ai-gateway.credits.autotopup_current_con: When credit balance reached $%{threshold}, add $%{amount} automatically.
ai-gateway.credits.spendlimit_sidebar_st: Tracks spends within set time interval.
Tracks spends over a moving time window.
ai-gateway.credits.spendlimit_descriptio: Prevent your AI Gateway from incurring unexpected charges by setting a spend limit. When the spend limit is reached, the AI Gateway will stop processing requests until the configured conditions are met.
ai-gateway.credits.spendlimit_moved: Spend limits are now per gateway. Remove this spend limit by disabling it.
ai-gateway.credits.spendlimit_email_mess: You will be notified via email when you reach 90% of your limit.
ai-gateway.credits.disable_spendlimit_mo: Spend limits are now managed per gateway. Disabling this removes your existing account-level limit and all spending restrictions on it.
ai-gateway.credits.disable_autotopup_mod: Disabling auto top-up may cause service interruptions if your credit balance runs low. You can re-enable it later.
ai-gateway.credits.payment_fees_disclaim: These credits can only be used for AI Gateway and by clicking "Confirm and Pay", you agree to our Terms and Privacy Policy.
ai-gateway.credits.autotopup_terms_confi: I have read, understood, and agree to be bound by the above terms.
ai-gateway.credits.credits_added: $%{amount} has been added to your account.
ai-gateway.credits.topup_may_take_a_minu: Applying credits can take a couple of minutes after payment.
ai-gateway.credits.polling_backend_slow: Your credits will appear shortly. Please refresh in a minute if they have not yet been applied.
ai-gateway.credits.polling_backend_unrea: We couldn't confirm with our billing system right now. Your payment was accepted; please refresh in a minute to see your credits.
ai-gateway.credits.default_payment_metho: We will use your default payment method to perform automatic recharges. Please ensure that your payment method supports automatic payments.
ai-gateway.credits.first_topup_disclaime: Please load credits to start using unified billing on AI Gateway.
ai-gateway.credits.threshold_confirmatio: The auto top-up threshold configured ($%{threshold}) is greater than your current balance ($%{balance}). Confirming will lead to your account instantly loading in credits automatically. Are you sure you want to proceed?
ai-gateway.credits.spendlimit_summary_da: Set at $%{amount} daily, using a fixed strategy.
Set at $%{amount} daily, using a sliding strategy.
ai-gateway.credits.spendlimit_summary_we: Set at $%{amount} weekly, using a fixed strategy.
Set at $%{amount} weekly, using a sliding strategy.
ai-gateway.credits.spendlimit_summary_mo: Set at $%{amount} monthly, using a fixed strategy.
Set at $%{amount} monthly, using a sliding strategy.
ai-gateway.credits.terms_disclaimer.auth: By enabling "Auto top-up" you agree to our Terms and authorize Cloudflare to use your designated payment method to automatically purchase pre-paid usage credits ("Usage Credits") to be added to your account in the increments you select when your balance reaches the threshold balance you configure (e.g., $0, $5, etc.).
ai-gateway.credits.terms_disclaimer.cont: Auto top-up will continue until disabled.
ai-gateway.credits.terms_disclaimer.opt_: You may disable Auto top-up at any time by selecting the toggle on the AI Gateway dashboard.
ai-gateway.credits.terms_disclaimer.pric: Usage Credits may only be used for AI Gateway's unified billing feature based on the then-current pricing posted at https://developers.cloudflare.com/ai-gateway/reference/pricing/, which may be updated from time-to-time at Cloudflare's discretion.
ai-gateway.credits.terms_disclaimer.expi: Usage Credits are non-refundable, non-transferable, and expire the earlier of (a) one calendar year from the date of first issuance by Cloudflare or (b) when your account or subscription is terminated for any reason.
ai-gateway.credits.terms_disclaimer.lega: Usage Credits are not legal tender or currency of any kind and have no cash value.
ai-gateway.contract_billing.label: Contract billing
ai-gateway.contract_billing.enterprise_n: This account has enterprise billing enabled. Contact your account manager for more information.
ai-gateway.contract_billing.top_up_disab: Top-up credits, spend limit, and auto recharge are not available.
ai-gateway.contract_billing.list_price_n: Prices shown are list prices. Contract discounts are applied by finance and may not be reflected here.
ai-gateway.custom_providers.title: Custom Providers
ai-gateway.custom_providers.subtitle: Custom providers allow you to use private providers / providers unsupported by default with AI Gateway. Provider keys are configured per gateway on each gateway’s Provider Keys tab.
ai-gateway.custom_providers.configure_ca: Bring private or unsupported AI providers into AI Gateway. Custom providers are account-wide; once configured, add a provider key for one on each gateway’s Provider Keys tab.
Custom providers are managed in account settings. Once a custom provider exists on your account, add a key for it on this page.
ai-gateway.custom_providers.base_url_htt: Base URL must start with https://
ai-gateway.custom_providers.empty_state: No custom providers found, add one to get started
ai-gateway.custom_providers.confirm_dele: Are you sure you want to delete this custom provider? This action cannot be undone.
ai-gateway.custom_providers.error_create: Could not create the custom provider. Check the details and try again.
ai-gateway.custom_providers.error_update: Could not update the custom provider. Check the details and try again.
ai-gateway.custom_providers.error_slug_c: A custom provider with this slug already exists on this account. Choose a different slug.
ai-gateway.custom_providers.error_delete: Could not delete the custom provider. Please try again.
ai-gateway.custom_providers.per_gateway_: Keys are configured per gateway. Manage them on each gateway’s Provider Keys tab.
No keys configured on any gateway for this custom provider.
Could not load keys for one or more gateways. Manage keys from each gateway’s Provider Keys tab.
ai-gateway.common.evaluators.cost.title: Cost
ai-gateway.common.evaluators.cost.descri: Calculate the average cost of requests (limited to requests that have supported costs).
ai-gateway.common.evaluators.speed.title: Speed
ai-gateway.common.evaluators.speed.descr: Calculate the average duration of requests.
ai-gateway.common.evaluators.feedback.de: Calculate the performance using human feedback annotated from logs (% of thumbs up).
ai-gateway.guardrails_tab.title: Firewall
ai-gateway.guardrails_tab.description: Inspect prompts and responses for unsafe content, prompt injection, and sensitive data before they reach your providers.
ai-gateway.domains_tab.title: Domains
ai-gateway.domains_tab.description: Route requests to this gateway through Cloudflare. Add a custom domain to put your own hostname in front of the gateway URL.
ai-gateway.domains_tab.validation_timing: Cloudflare is issuing an SSL certificate for this domain. Automatic validation usually completes within 10 - 15 minutes, but can take more than 24 hours. If it's still pending after 4 hours, verify the CNAME is in place and proxied. <0>Learn about validation timing</0>
ai-gateway.domains_tab.dns_ready_toast_d: %{hostname} now points at the AI Gateway edge. Automatic SSL validation usually completes within 10 - 15 minutes, but can take more than 24 hours — the row updates automatically.
ai-gateway.domains_tab.status_error_tool: AI Gateway couldn't provision this domain and won't retry. Common causes: the hostname is already in use elsewhere on Cloudflare, the zone is no longer on this account, or a bad AI Gateway binding in <0>wrangler.toml</0>. Fix the underlying issue, then remove and re-add the domain.
ai-gateway.domains_tab.status_initializi: Registering the domain with AI Gateway. This takes a few seconds.
ai-gateway.domains_tab.status_pending_dn: Waiting for the CNAME at your DNS provider. Once it’s live, Cloudflare finishes issuing the SSL certificate and this row flips to Enabled automatically.
ai-gateway.domains_tab.partial_zone_setu: This zone uses partial (CNAME) setup, so Cloudflare doesn't serve DNS for it. After you click Add domain we'll show the CNAME to paste at your DNS provider — SSL provisioning starts once the record is live. <0>Learn about validation timing</0>
ai-gateway.domains_tab.setup_dns_descrip: Almost there. Add the CNAME below at your DNS provider and this domain will finish provisioning on its own.
Add the CNAME below at your DNS provider and this domain will finish provisioning on its own.
ai-gateway.domains_tab.partial_dns_intro: The zone this hostname sits on isn't authoritatively served by Cloudflare, so we can't add the record for you. Add this proxied CNAME at the DNS provider that hosts <0>%{hostname}</0>:
ai-gateway.domains_tab.partial_dns_foote: Once the CNAME is live, Cloudflare finishes issuing an SSL certificate for this domain in the background. The status here updates automatically — you don't need to wait on this page. If it's still pending after about 15 minutes, double-check the record is proxied. <0>Learn about validation timing</0>
ai-gateway.domains_tab.no_modify_permiss: You don’t have permission to modify this gateway.
ai-gateway.access_tab.title: Access
ai-gateway.access_tab.description: Control who can reach this gateway with granular authentication.
ai-gateway.access_tab.no_domain_descript: Access protects requests to a custom domain in front of this gateway. Add a domain in the Domains tab, then come back here to layer Access policies on top.
ai-gateway.provider_keys.title: Provider Keys
ai-gateway.provider_keys.subtitle: Securely use your own provider API keys with AI Gateway without the hassle of managing them in code.
ai-gateway.provider_keys.auth_warning: Your gateway needs to be authenticated to store and use keys.
ai-gateway.provider_keys.help.example_de: With stored keys, no Authorization header needed. The key with the "default" alias is used automatically:
ai-gateway.provider_keys.help.example_no: Your provider API key is injected at runtime.
ai-gateway.provider_keys.help.alias_desc: To use a different key, include the alias header:
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.secret_st: Your key is encrypted and stored securely using Secrets Store.
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.alias_hel: A label that identifies this key. The “default” alias is used automatically; use named aliases (e.g. “production”) when you need multiple keys for the same provider on this gateway.
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.alias_pla: Enter a unique alias (e.g., production, development)
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.alias_des: A unique identifier for this key. Cannot be changed after creation.
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.alias_too: For non-default aliases, pass the header "cf-aig-byok-alias: <alias_name>" in the request to specify which key alias to use.
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.alias_dup: This alias already exists for this provider
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.alias_inv: Alias can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.google_ve: Configure your Google Vertex AI credentials.
Paste your Google Cloud service account JSON here...
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.azure_ope: Enter your Azure OpenAI resource name
ai-gateway.edit_provider_modal.aws_bedro: Enter your AWS Access Key ID
Enter your AWS Secret Access Key
Use AWS IAM credentials for SigV4 authentication with Bedrock.
ai-gateway.confirm_provider_config_delet: Are you sure you want to delete the key for %{provider}?
Are you sure you want to delete the "%{alias}" key for %{provider}?
Deleting this key will cause all requests to %{provider} that depend on it to fail.
